subject: expat retirees
one_liner: Cross-border payments for people who told everyone the move was temporary.
brand_voice_notes: A correspondence bank that writes to you like it knew your father. Sincere fintech pitch delivered by a founder who has never met a retiree but has priced one. Confident about fees, vague about timelines. Treats emigration as a liquidity event and homesickness as a billing category. Never winks.

hero.h1: Your money can also refuse to come home.
hero.sub: Settle. moves your pension, your birthday transfers, and your outstanding bar tabs across borders at rates we have described, in writing, as fair.

features.title: Legacy Rates
features.blurb: Send money at the exchange rate from the year you emigrated. The difference accrues quietly and is invoiced to your estate, addressed to whichever child answers.

features.title: Grandchild Rails
features.blurb: Recurring birthday transfers with a handwriting-simulated memo line. Names, ages, and preferred currencies tracked on your behalf, so the affection is fully automated.

features.title: Tab Consolidation
features.blurb: We merge your open tabs at up to four expat bars into one monthly statement, denominated in whichever currency performed best. For you or for us, the statement doesn't say.

pricing_tiers.name: Tourist Visa
pricing_tiers.tagline: For people still calling it a long holiday.

pricing_tiers.name: Golden Visa
pricing_tiers.tagline: You bought the apartment. Buy the fees.
perks: A dedicated account officer named Nigel

pricing_tiers.name: Repatriation
pricing_tiers.price: One lump sum, discussed over lunch
pricing_tiers.tagline: For estates, executors, and children acting on power of attorney.

testimonials.quote: The birthday transfer arrived on his actual birthday. My grandson thinks I'm reliable now, which is new.
testimonials.name: Graham Whitmore
testimonials.title: Former Regional Claims Adjuster, currently 'the British one' at a beach bar in Hua Hin

testimonials.quote: I locked in the 2011 rate. I don't know what it's costing me, and Settle. has been very firm that I shouldn't.
testimonials.name: Birgit Halvorsen
testimonials.title: Retired School Inspector, seventeen years into a two-year stay in Alicante

testimonials.quote: They consolidated four bar tabs into one invoice. My cardiologist and my accountant now receive the same document.
testimonials.name: Dennis Okafor
testimonials.title: Ex-Procurement Director, Chairman of an Unofficial Domino Club, Playa del Carmen

images.rationale: hero skipped because the direction is typographic letterhead editorial; demo assets skipped because the demo is a ledger/console (not photo-shaped); full budget minus one went to 3 testimonial passport-photo headshots, which the stamped-testimonial treatment is built around

items.prompt: Deadpan passport-style corporate headshot photograph of a British retired man around 70, sunburnt pink complexion, thinning grey combover, wearing a faded tropical beach-bar shirt with a too-formal collar, staring straight at the camera with bureaucratic neutrality, flat even lighting, plain warm cream studio backdrop in sun-faded off-white #F6EFDF, muted vintage film tones. One focal subject only. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan passport-style corporate headshot photograph of a Scandinavian retired woman around 72, silver hair in a severe practical bob, deep tan from years of Spanish sun, wearing crisp white linen and small pearl earrings, staring straight at the camera with the stern neutrality of a school inspector, flat even lighting, plain warm cream studio backdrop in sun-faded off-white #F6EFDF, muted vintage film tones. One focal subject only. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan passport-style corporate headshot photograph of a Nigerian retired man around 68, short white hair and neat white moustache, wearing an immaculate pale embroidered guayabera shirt, reading glasses pushed up on his head, staring straight at the camera with the settled authority of a former procurement director, flat even lighting, plain warm cream studio backdrop in sun-faded off-white #F6EFDF, muted vintage film tones. One focal subject only. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

demo_screen_concept: A 'Send It Home' transfers console: left panel is a converter where you type an amount in euros and it renders the destination currency plus two vanity units (pints at your old local vs pints at your new local); center is the grandchild birthday queue with countdown chips and a favorite-reorder control; right is a pending-transfers feed where everything sits in 'Clearing (6–8 weeks)' with a Nudge button that increments a nudge counter and changes nothing.

design_direction.style: Editorial airmail-stationery correspondence bank, sun-faded Riviera daylight mode. The page is staged as the printed brochure of a heavily funded cross-border payments company that writes to its customers as though it knew their fathers, set entirely on airmail envelope stationery. A warm cream paper ground carries a faint laid-paper feel via subtle repeating linear striations; the classic par-avion border, alternating postal-blue and airmail-red diagonal stripes, runs as a thin band along the top of the page and as section dividers, so the whole site reads as one long envelope rather than a webpage. Panels are letter plates: flat cream fills a shade lighter than the ground, 1px postal-blue hairline borders, 2px radii, no shadows deeper than a faint paper lift. Pricing cards wear postage-stamp perforated edges (radial-gradient punch holes along the border) and a denomination in the corner like a stamp value. Rubber-stamp motifs do the state-keeping: a circular postmark with the founding date sits by the wordmark, and skewed rubber-stamp marks (CLEARED 6–8 WEEKS, RECEIVED, PRIORITY) in faded red ink annotate transfers and testimonials, always rotated 2–4 degrees, never straight. One postal blue carries the wordmark, headlines' punctuation, hairlines, structural labels, and every institutional surface; one rationed airmail red, the red of the envelope stripe, is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA per section, the live clearing-status dot, and the rubber stamps, and appears almost nowhere else as fill. Fraunces sets the wordmark and headlines in warm old-style serif with the letterpress confidence of a bank that predates its customers; Libre Franklin carries plain, faintly clerical body copy. Every figure that pretends to be remittance telemetry (exchange rates, clearing windows, fees, pint conversions, tier prices, nudge counts) is set in Courier Prime, the typewriter face of a branch that never upgraded, with tabular alignment inside ruled ledger wells. Motion is philatelic and restrained: rubber stamps thump in once on scroll (scale 1.15 to 1 with a small rotation settle), the clearing dot pulses on a 3s cycle, letter plates rise 6px and fade in on scroll, the CTA depresses like a hand stamp on press, the demo's typewriter figures tick digit by digit, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No dark mode, no glass, no blur, no clay, no neon, no bento lattice, no brutalist offset shadows, no marquee, no emoji. Reads like a bank that files homesickness under 'foreign exchange exposure' and lists your children as beneficiaries-in-waiting.

fonts.body: 'Libre Franklin', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: correspondence-bank brochure: letterhead nav with postmark seal, letter-opening hero (salutation kicker, giant serif h1, typed sub), par-avion striped dividers between sections, 3-up letter-plate features, stamped testimonials with headshots as passport photos, perforated stamp pricing cards, footer as envelope flap
design_direction.motion_vocab: rubber-stamp thump-ins (scale+rotation settle, once), 3s pulsing clearing dot, 6px rise-and-fade on scroll, hand-stamp CTA depress on press, typewriter digit ticks in the demo, all behind prefers-reduced-motion
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Kicker line 'PAR AVION · EST. WHEREVER YOU LEFT' in letterspaced Libre Franklin caps between two airmail stripe bands; h1 in huge Fraunces across two lines; sub set as a typed paragraph with a Courier Prime rate line beneath it ('TODAY'S RATE: 1.000 EUR = 1.000 EUR, LESS OUR PORTION'); single red CTA styled as a rubber stamp button; a faded circular postmark overlapping the h1's corner at 4 degrees rotation.
